component.name: TuxVox
component.summary: Speech to text — dictate and transcribe with ease

description.p: TuxVox is a stable, CPU-only speech-to-text application for Linux, built from a clean-slate architecture using GTK4 and libadwaita. It supports multiple Whisper models, letting you choose the right balance between speed and accuracy for your hardware. Text flows onto the screen in a smooth typewriter style as your speech is transcribed in real time. Everything runs fully offline on your machine — no cloud services, no data collection — so your voice stays private.

🔒 100% Private and Offline — TuxVox uses a local AI model (OpenAI Whisper) that runs entirely on your computer. Your voice is processed locally, never transmitted to any server, and your voice audio is never stored after transcription is complete. The only network request TuxVox makes is an optional one-time model download. After that, it works with no internet connection — permanently.
v0.2: Experimental Mode with Global Hotkey and Inline Typing, plus privacy transparency messaging throughout the app.
